title: Java HashMap
content:
### HashMap in Java

HashMap stores key-value pairs. It uses hashing to provide O(1) average time for get and put operations.

---

### Creating a HashMap

```java
import java.util.HashMap;

HashMap<String, Integer> ages = new HashMap<>();
HashMap<String, String> capitals = new HashMap<>(100);  // initial capacity
HashMap<String, String> copy = new HashMap<>(otherMap);
```

---

### Common Methods

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `put(key, value)` | Insert or update |
| `get(key)` | Get value by key (null if absent) |
| `getOrDefault(key, default)` | Get value or default |
| `remove(key)` | Remove entry by key |
| `containsKey(key)` | Check if key exists |
| `containsValue(value)` | Check if value exists |
| `keySet()` | Set of all keys |
| `values()` | Collection of values |
| `entrySet()` | Set of key-value entries |
| `size()` | Number of entries |
| `clear()` | Remove all entries |

---

### Iterating a HashMap

```java
for (String key : map.keySet()) {
    System.out.println(key + " -> " + map.get(key));
}

for (Map.Entry<String, Integer> entry : map.entrySet()) {
    System.out.println(entry.getKey() + " = " + entry.getValue());
}
```

---

### HashMap Characteristics

- Permits one null key and multiple null values
- Not synchronized (use ConcurrentHashMap for thread safety)
- No guaranteed iteration order
- Initial capacity 16, load factor 0.75

> **Key Point:** HashMap uses `hashCode()` and `equals()` to locate and identify keys. Always override both methods properly for custom key objects.

quiz.question: What is the average time complexity of HashMap get/put?
How many null keys can a HashMap have?
